item.title: Intestinal ultrasound to detect postoperative recurrence in patients with Crohn's disease: an international, multidisciplinary, RAND/UCLA appropriateness method study.
item.description: The lancet. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Intestinal ultrasound is a non-invasive method increasingly used to diagnose and monitor disease activity in patients with inflammatory bowel disease; however, there is no consensus on the appropri...

item.title: Extracellular volume fraction derived from dual-energy CT characterizing the fibrosis of the bowel wall in stricturing Crohn disease.
item.description: Japanese journal of radiology - To prospectively evaluate the ability of extracellular volume (ECV) fraction, derived from dual-energy CT (DECT), to characterize fibrosis in bowel wall in participants with Crohn disease (CD). Rou...

item.title: Early Predictors of Steroid Failure in Acute Severe Ulcerative Colitis: A Retrospective Study With the Evaluation of the Admission Model for Intensification of Therapy in Acute Severe Colitis (ADMIT-ASC) Score.
item.description: Cureus - Acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC) is a life-threatening gastroenterological emergency in which early identification of patients at risk of corticosteroid failure is critical for timely therape...

item.title: Review of Current Medical and Surgical Therapy for Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of clinical gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease, comprised of Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a chronic, immune-mediated disorder of the gastrointestinal tract. The therapeutic landscape for IBD ha...

item.title: Tissue transcriptional programs stratify Crohn's disease by therapeutic response and clinical outcomes.
item.description: Scientific reports - Crohn's disease is biologically heterogeneous, and current clinical classifications poorly predict treatment response or disease progression. The molecular programs that drive clinical outcomes in ...

item.title: Meflin confers antifibrotic properties to intestinal fibroblasts in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: The Journal of clinical investigation - Dysfunctional intestinal fibrosis is an irreversible complication of Crohn's disease (CD), The complex heterogeneity of intestinal mesenchymal cells makes it difficult to understand the pathogenesi...

item.title: Rituximab-associated colitis with colonic perforation requiring subtotal colectomy in systemic sclerosis.
item.description: Clinical journal of gastroenterology - Rituximab-associated colitis, a rare toxicity that may occur following prolonged latency, has nonspecific features. We report a 42-year-old female with diffuse cutaneous systemic sclerosis and inte...

item.title: Isolated Rectal Polypoid Lesion as the Initial Presentation of Crohn's Disease.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Crohn's disease can present with atypical and isolated manifestations, contributing to diagnostic delays. We describe a 45-year-old woman undergoing routine colorectal cancer screening who was foun...

item.title: Stricturing Phenotype Predisposes to Small Bowel and Colorectal Malignancy in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: JGH open : an open access journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Carcinogenesis is a major complication in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). We report the data of Crohn's disease (CD)-associated intestinal malignancy in our IBD cohort. Retrospectiv...

item.title: LONG-TERM QUALITY OF LIFE AFTER ILEAL POUCH-ANAL ANASTOMOSIS FOR ULCERATIVE COLITIS IN A BRAZILIAN IBD REFERRAL CENTER.
item.description: Arquivos de gastroenterologia - Reports on the long-term impact on quality of life (QoL) after ileal pouch-anal anastomosis (IPAA) for ulcerative colitis (UC) are scarce in Latin America. To evaluate QoL after IPAA in an inflamma...

item.title: Fecal incontinence and defecatory dyssynergia after ileoanal pouch in ulcerative colitis: frequently underdiagnosed complications.
Efficacy analysis of the Turnbull-Cutait procedure in refractory perianal Crohn's disease: a two-centre retrospective study.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - Patients with refractory perianal Crohn's disease (RpCD) often face rectal resection and permanent ostomy as a final resort. The Turnbull-Cutait (TC) pull-through procedure offers a potential sphin...

item.title: Case Report: Exploring the link between severe gingivitis and inflammatory bowel disease in a young patient.
item.description: Frontiers in dental medicine - Case report - Severe gingival inflammation, which is uncommon in pediatric patients, often indicates underlying systemic conditions. Leukemia, autoimmune diseases, and allergic symptoms can cause gingivitis owin...
